meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.
meta.blueprintRefinement: THIS BLUEPRINT MUST BE CONTINUALLY REFINED. After completing any task, before starting the next, re-read this file and assess whether it still accurately captures reality. If any task description is stale, wrong, unclear, or missing context — FIX IT NOW.

globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use config.mk custom targets. Limit output. Pipe through tail after tee to build.log. Use 'make find-test-failures'.
CROSS-PLATFORM: ALL checks must pass on macOS, Linux (Docker), Windows (host 'moo'). Zero tolerance.
TIMING: Tests MUST NOT be timing-dependent. No long-running tests. Race detector must pass.
SESSION TIMER: Started 2026-02-20T08:21:58Z. 9h mandate. Verify: make check-session-time.
RULE OF TWO: Two contiguous issue-free subagent reviews before any commit. Same diff. No exceptions.
DEADCODE: NEVER add ignores to .deadcodeignore. Wire up the code or delete it.
BUILD: Run 'make make-all-with-log' after EVERY code change. Zero failures before proceeding.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: One task at a time, in order. Do not attempt to solve all tasks in a single turn.
mandatoryDirectives.subagentPolicy: Use subagents for Rule of Two review gate ONLY. Work subagents do NOT self-review.
mandatoryDirectives.blueprintRefinement: Continual refinement required. After every task, re-read and fix stale descriptions.
mandatoryDirectives.runMakeAfterEveryChange: Run 'make make-all-with-log' after EVERY code change. Zero failures before proceeding.
mandatoryDirectives.commitPolicy: Commit in logical chunks after passing Rule of Two. Do NOT accumulate massive diffs.

statusSection.currentState: T101-T103 Done. Build GREEN. Proceeding to T104.
statusSection.priorCompletedWork: T001-T029 completed in prior sessions. See CHANGELOG.md and WIP.md for details. Removed unwired readable_stream.go (will be re-created properly in T105).

sequentialTasks.task: T101: Fix txtar context root formatting
sequentialTasks.description: Fixed in ctxutil.go buildContext(): metadata lines (context root, common path, tracked directories) are now extracted and rendered OUTSIDE the code fence with paths in backticks. 3 new test cases (MetadataExtracted, NoMetadata, MetadataOnly). Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T102: Fix txtar path ambiguity in computeUniqueSuffixes
sequentialTasks.description: VERIFIED: All 3 bug scenarios already fixed + tested in context_txtar_lca_test.go from prior sessions. (1) LCA computed and emitted in txtar Comment via computePathLCA — 7 test cases. (2) All-relative collision groups use full relative paths (allRelative check, false proximity fix) — 2 test cases. (3) Non-colliding basenames preserve full paths for relative entries — 2 test cases. Removed duplicate tests from state_coverage_gaps_test.go. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T103: Fix MCP auto-config to use command/stdio instead of URL/HTTP
sequentialTasks.description: DONE: Rewrote MCPInstanceConfig to use command/args format (stdio transport) instead of URL/HTTP. Removed: ListenAndServe, Endpoint, ListenerAddr, ErrNotListening, ErrAlreadyListening, endpointType, HTTP server, Unix socket. Added: OsmBinary field (defaults to os.Executable()), command/args in mcpServerEntry. Updated JS bindings in module.go (removed HTTP methods, added osmBinary). Updated instance.go state writing. 15 tests covering new format. Build GREEN.

sequentialTasks.task: T104: Implement osm mcp-instance subcommand (stdio MCP server)
sequentialTasks.description: Create a new subcommand 'osm mcp-instance' that runs an MCP server over stdio (stdin/stdout) for a specific session. Accept --session flag. Reuse existing MCP tools from internal/command/mcp.go but with stdio transport instead of HTTP. Register in main.go. Add tests. Shell completion. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T105: Implement ReadableStream type for fetch Response.body
sequentialTasks.description: Re-create internal/builtin/fetch/readable_stream.go: ReadableStream backed by io.ReadCloser with bounded channel (back-pressure). Implement getReader() returning ReadableStreamDefaultReader, locked property, cancel(). Pump goroutine with lazy init. MUST be wired into fetch.go Response object. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T106: Implement reader.read() with Promise resolution
sequentialTasks.description: ReadableStreamDefaultReader.read() must return Promise<{value: string, done: boolean}>. Each call reads one chunk from bounded channel. Use adapter.JS().NewChainedPromise() for proper async. releaseLock() releases reader. Wire into Response.body. Add unit tests for read/done/cancel/releaseLock. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T107: Implement SSE parser for ReadableStream
sequentialTasks.description: Create sseParser that consumes ReadableStream chunks and emits parsed SSEEvent structs (event, data, id, retry). Parse per W3C SSE spec: double-newline delimiters, colon field parsing, comment lines. Handle \r\n and \n. Expose as JS reader object with read() returning Promise. Add comprehensive tests with httptest SSE server. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T108: End-to-end Goja integration tests for ReadableStream + SSE
sequentialTasks.description: Write E2E tests that exercise ReadableStream from JS: (1) fetch() a chunked endpoint, read body via getReader(), verify chunks arrive. (2) fetch() an SSE endpoint, parse events via SSE reader. (3) Test back-pressure by slow consumer. (4) Test cancel() mid-stream. Use httptest for server. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T109: macOS process hierarchy fallback for session ID
sequentialTasks.description: Create session_darwin.go (//go:build darwin): use sysctl/libproc for process tree traversal, skip ephemeral wrappers, detect shell/root boundaries, use PID+start-time for stability. Update session_other.go build tag to exclude darwin. Update docs/session.md. Write tests using mock process trees. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T110: Implement osm claudemux run — PTY subprocess spawning
sequentialTasks.description: CRITICAL: Create native Go command 'osm claudemux run' that actually spawns Claude Code (or other TUI tools) as PTY subprocesses. Read task list from stdin or directory. Use claudemux.Pool for instance management. Use claudemux.SpawnOpts for configuration. Wire creack/pty for PTY allocation. Register in main.go. This replaces the inert 'claude-mux start'. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T111: Wire Pool dispatch into claudemux run
sequentialTasks.description: Connect the Pool's acquire/release/dispatch cycle to actual PTY instances in 'claudemux run'. Implement round-robin task assignment. Health tracking via PTY output monitoring. Graceful drain on shutdown. Tests with mock PTY processes. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T112: Wire Panel TUI into claudemux run
sequentialTasks.description: Launch claudemux.Panel as the interactive TUI for 'claudemux run'. Alt+1..9 switching between instances. Per-pane scrollback. Health indicators in status bar. PgUp/PgDown navigation. Verify no tmux keybinding conflicts. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T113: Integration tests for claudemux run end-to-end
sequentialTasks.description: Write integration tests for the full 'claudemux run' pipeline: spawn mock processes, dispatch tasks, verify Pool allocation, verify Panel renders output, verify graceful shutdown. Use simulated CI tests (no real Claude needed). Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T114: Session interlock — TUI and MCP synchronization
sequentialTasks.description: Wire PTY's NavigateToModel into 'osm claudemux run'. Ensure TUI renders prompts dynamically. Autosolve model selection. Synchronize MCP handshake via heartbeat verification. Test MCP session coordination with TUI lifecycle. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T115: Implement osm mcp make subcommand
sequentialTasks.description: Port mcp-server-make functionality: implement 'osm mcp make' that provides MCP tools for running Make targets. Target caching, cross-platform make detection (make vs gmake), session integration. MCP tool: make_run with target and workdir params. Shell completion for make targets. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T116: Agent steering — session control socket (Unix/TCP)
sequentialTasks.description: Session-tied Unix socket (gRPC), Windows TCP fallback. Proto definitions: EnqueueTask, InterruptCurrent, GetStatus. Socket path: ~/.osm/sessions/<id>/control.sock. Proper cleanup on session end. Tests with in-process gRPC server. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T117: Agent steering — gRPC service implementation
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the gRPC service for EnqueueTask (adds task to session queue), InterruptCurrent (sends cancellation to active task), GetStatus (returns session state, active task, queue depth). Wire into session lifecycle. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T118: Implement osm mcp parent — Claude Code wrapper
sequentialTasks.description: Create 'osm mcp parent' command: wrap Claude Code child process with stdio MCP transport. Session socket messaging (T116-T117). Lifecycle management: spawn, monitor, restart on crash. MCP tools: enqueue, interrupt, status. Tests with mock Claude process. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T119: Implement osm mcp prompt — polymorphic terminal wrapper
sequentialTasks.description: Create 'osm mcp prompt': Anthropic computer use research (document findings first). Dynamic tool availability based on context. BubbleTea UI for interactive prompting. Mode detection (interactive vs pipe). go-prompt REPL integration. Adaptive MCP schema generation. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T120: Cross-platform checkpoint — post MCP/claudemux activation
sequentialTasks.description: Verify ALL packages pass on macOS + Linux (Docker via make-all-in-container) + Windows (host moo via make-all-run-windows). Zero failures. Fix any regressions from T101-T119. Run make on all platforms.

sequentialTasks.task: T121: CHANGELOG — MCP and claudemux activation milestone
sequentialTasks.description: Update CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] with entries for T101-T119. Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0 format. Added/Changed/Fixed sections. Verify format. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T122: go-git v6 dependency setup
sequentialTasks.description: Add github.com/go-git/go-git/v6 to go.mod. Verify compatibility. Create internal/gitops/gogit.go with thin wrapper around go-git API for clone, add, commit, push, pull operations. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T123: Migrate sync.go to go-git for clone/add/commit/push
sequentialTasks.description: Replace exec.Command('git', ...) shell-outs in sync.go with go-git v6 calls for: clone, add, commit, push. Preserve: push commits with 'osm sync: <RFC3339>' message. Preserve sync.local-path config key. EXCEPTION: Keep shell-out for pull --rebase (go-git rebase support is limited). Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T124: Migrate sync_startup.go to go-git for auto-pull
sequentialTasks.description: Replace exec.Command('git', 'pull', '--rebase') in sync_startup.go with go-git where feasible. Preserve: non-blocking auto-pull on startup, conflict detection from stderr. If go-git rebase is unreliable, keep as shell-out with documentation. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T125: go-git migration — integration tests with real repos
sequentialTasks.description: Write integration tests that clone to temp dirs, add files, commit, push to bare repos, pull with rebase. Verify conflict detection still works. Verify timestamp messages. Verify sync.local-path. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T126: Anthropic prompt library expansion — 3 new goals
sequentialTasks.description: Add 3 new built-in goals to goal_builtin.go from Anthropic prompt library NOT already implemented (existing: morale-improver, bug-buster, code-optimizer, code-explainer, meeting-notes, pii-scrubber, prose-polisher, compare-options). Each goal needs: stateVars, hotSnippets, flagDefs, promptOptions. Integration tests for each. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T127: Security audit — require() path traversal
sequentialTasks.description: Audit require() in the Goja runtime for directory traversal attacks. Verify that require('../../etc/passwd') and similar paths are blocked. Add regression tests for all known traversal patterns (../, symlinks, null bytes). Fix any vulnerabilities. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T128: Security audit — exec injection
sequentialTasks.description: Audit osm:exec and osm:pty modules for command injection. Verify that shell metacharacters in arguments are properly escaped. Test with payloads: semicolons, pipes, backticks, $(), etc. Fix any vulnerabilities. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T129: Security audit — fetch SSRF prevention
sequentialTasks.description: Audit osm:fetch for SSRF vulnerabilities. Verify that localhost, 169.254.169.254 (AWS metadata), internal IPs, file:// URLs are blocked or configurable. Add tests. Fix vulnerabilities. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T130: Security audit — environment variable secrets
sequentialTasks.description: Audit all points where environment variables are exposed to JS scripts. Verify sensitive vars (API keys, tokens, passwords) are filtered. Check MCP privilege escalation vectors. Update docs/security.md with complete threat model. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T131: Security audit — os module write restrictions
sequentialTasks.description: Audit osm:os module for unrestricted filesystem writes. Verify sandbox boundaries. Test symlink escape, /tmp races, write to sensitive paths. Fix vulnerabilities. Regression tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T132: Module resolution refinement
sequentialTasks.description: Review and refine the JS module resolution strategy (script.module-paths config key, require() in Goja). Ensure consistent behavior: relative paths resolve from script dir, absolute paths work, module-paths are searched in order. Document the resolution algorithm in docs/scripting.md. Add edge case tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T133: Configuration model audit and cleanup
sequentialTasks.description: Audit all config keys in internal/config/schema.go. Verify each key is: (1) documented in docs/reference/config.md, (2) actually read and used in the codebase, (3) has correct default values, (4) has validation. Remove any dead config keys. Add missing documentation. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T134: go-prompt integration enhancement
sequentialTasks.description: Review and enhance the go-prompt integration in internal/scripting/. Verify feature parity between registerMode and createPrompt paths. Test dynamic completion, key bindings, color support. Ensure cross-platform terminal compatibility. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T135: Code review splitter refinement
sequentialTasks.description: Review and refine the code review splitter in internal/command/diff_splitter.go. Ensure it handles all git diff formats correctly. Test with large diffs, binary files, renames, mode changes. Improve splitting heuristics. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T136: Cross-platform checkpoint — post expansion
sequentialTasks.description: Verify ALL packages pass on macOS + Linux (Docker) + Windows (moo). Zero failures. Fix regressions from T122-T135. Run make on all platforms.

sequentialTasks.task: T137: Test coverage gap analysis
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make cover'. Generate coverage report. Identify ALL packages below 90%. List specific functions/methods with missing coverage. Produce coverage-gaps.md in scratch/ with actionable items. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T138: Coverage remediation — internal/command package
sequentialTasks.description: Address coverage gaps in internal/command/. Focus on: error paths, edge cases, platform-specific code, concurrency scenarios. Add tests for every uncovered function. Target 100%. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T139: Coverage remediation — internal/scripting package
sequentialTasks.description: Address coverage gaps in internal/scripting/. Focus on: TUI lifecycle, event loop edge cases, module loading errors, runtime panics. Add tests. Target 100%. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T140: Coverage remediation — internal/builtin packages
sequentialTasks.description: Address coverage gaps across ALL internal/builtin/ sub-packages (claudemux, fetch, exec, pty, bt, etc.). Focus on error paths, boundary conditions, nil inputs. Add tests. Target 100%. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T141: Coverage remediation — internal/storage and internal/session
sequentialTasks.description: Address coverage gaps in storage and session packages. Test file locking edge cases, concurrent access, cleanup race conditions, backend switching. Target 100%. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T142: Coverage remediation — remaining packages
sequentialTasks.description: Address coverage gaps in: internal/config, internal/gitops, internal/argv, internal/termui, internal/testutil, cmd/osm. Target 100% across all packages. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T143: Shell completion audit — all commands and subcommands
sequentialTasks.description: Audit shell completion for ALL commands/subcommands in bash/zsh/fish/powershell. List every command and verify completion is registered. Fix gaps. Test output format for each shell. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T144: Documentation — README.md comprehensive update
sequentialTasks.description: Update root README.md: full feature set, claude-mux system, MCP server (both HTTP and stdio), JS modules list, goal system, sync, sessions, shell completion. Badges. Installation. Quick-start. Verify ALL links work. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T145: Documentation — docs/README.md index completeness
sequentialTasks.description: Review docs/README.md: ensure every document in docs/ is indexed. Cross-references must be valid. docs/reference/ must be complete for ALL commands and ALL config keys. Add missing entries. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T146: Documentation — docs/reference/ completeness
sequentialTasks.description: Audit docs/reference/: verify reference docs exist for every command, every config key, every JS module (osm:fetch, osm:exec, osm:pty, osm:flag, osm:ctxutil, osm:claudemux, etc.). Add missing reference docs. Verify accuracy of existing ones. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T147: Documentation — docs/scripting.md update
sequentialTasks.description: Update docs/scripting.md: complete JS module listing, module resolution algorithm, event loop behavior, Promise support, require() patterns, global objects (ctx, output, log, tui). Add examples for each module. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T148: Documentation — docs/security.md comprehensive update
sequentialTasks.description: Update docs/security.md with findings from T127-T131 security audit. Complete threat model. Document sandbox boundaries. Document allowed/blocked operations. Document MCP security model. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T149: Deadcode audit
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make lint' focusing on deadcode checker output. Fix ALL issues. Remove unused functions, types, variables. NO .deadcodeignore changes — wire up or delete. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T150: Betteralign audit
sequentialTasks.description: Run betteralign across ALL packages. Fix ALL struct field alignment issues. Verify no performance regressions. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T151: Staticcheck audit — resolve all warnings
sequentialTasks.description: Run staticcheck across ALL packages. Resolve every single warning (S1017, SA*, QF*, etc.). No suppressions. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T152: API surface cleanup — internal/builtin/claudemux
sequentialTasks.description: Review all exported types and functions in claudemux package. Remove any that are unused or misdesigned. Consolidate overlapping APIs. Ensure JS bindings match Go API 1:1. Remove deprecated aliases. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T153: API surface cleanup — internal/command
sequentialTasks.description: Review all exported types and functions in command package. Remove deprecated code. Consolidate command registration patterns. Ensure consistent error handling across all commands. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T154: API surface cleanup — internal/scripting
sequentialTasks.description: Review all exported types and functions in scripting package. Remove unused exports. Ensure TUI manager API is clean. Verify event loop integration points. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T155: Error handling audit
sequentialTasks.description: Audit ALL packages for: swallowed errors, missing error returns, panics instead of error returns, inconsistent error wrapping. Fix all issues. Ensure proper exit codes from CLI commands. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T156: Performance benchmarks — expand coverage
sequentialTasks.description: Review existing benchmarks in benchmark_test.go files. Add benchmarks for: fetch HTTP client, session locking, config parsing, goal loading, diff splitting, context building, txtar generation. Use ReportAllocs. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T157: Fuzz testing expansion
sequentialTasks.description: Review existing fuzz tests. Add fuzz tests for: config parsing, goal JSON loading, txtar generation, diff splitting, shell completion, MCP message parsing, SSE parsing. Ensure all fuzz seeds pass. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T158: Race condition audit
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'go test -race ./...' with focus on concurrent scenarios. Add race-condition-specific tests for: session locking, Pool dispatch, Panel concurrent updates, MCP session coordination, cleanup scheduler. Fix any races. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T159: Memory and resource leak audit
sequentialTasks.description: Audit all goroutine-spawning code for leaks. Verify all io.Closers are closed. Check for channel leaks. Add tests with runtime.NumGoroutine() assertions where appropriate. Fix leaks. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T160: Cross-platform checkpoint — post quality gates
sequentialTasks.description: Verify ALL packages pass on macOS + Linux (Docker) + Windows (moo). Zero failures. Fix regressions from T137-T159. Run make on all platforms.

sequentialTasks.task: T161: CHANGELOG — quality and expansion milestone
sequentialTasks.description: Update CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] with entries for T122-T159. Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0 format. Added/Changed/Fixed/Security sections. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T162: MacosUseSDK integration research
sequentialTasks.description: Research github.com/joeycumines/MacosUseSDK. Document capabilities. Assess feasibility of integration via in-process gRPC channel. Write findings to docs/archive/notes/macos-use-sdk-integration.md. If feasible, outline implementation tasks and add to blueprint. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T163: PR split behavior tree refinement
sequentialTasks.description: Review orchestrate-pr-split.js and orchestrate-pr-split.json. Verify BT nodes execute correctly. Test failure recovery paths. Ensure equivalence verification step works. Add tests for BT leaf nodes. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T164: Provider abstraction for multi-tool support
sequentialTasks.description: Design and implement provider abstraction layer for claudemux to support multiple TUI tools (Claude Code, gh CLI, gemini CLI, codium CLI). Define ProviderConfig interface. Implement ClaudeCodeProvider. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T165: Ollama validation integration
sequentialTasks.description: Implement Ollama HTTP client for local validation (osm:ollama module or similar). Connect to localhost:11434. Chat completion API. Streaming responses. Connection health checking. Use for safety validation in claudemux. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T166: Environment variable management for subprocess spawning
sequentialTasks.description: Implement three-tier env management: (1) inherit osm env, (2) script hooks for injection (op plugin, aws-vault), (3) profile-based config. Wire into claudemux spawn. Tests. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T167: Session cleanup scheduler hardening
sequentialTasks.description: Review CleanupScheduler implementation. Verify all 5 SessionConfig fields are properly respected (AutoCleanupEnabled, CleanupIntervalHours, MaxAgeDays, MaxCount, MaxSizeMB). Add edge case tests: zero values, negative values, concurrent cleanup. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T168: docs/architecture.md refresh
sequentialTasks.description: Update docs/architecture.md to reflect current codebase: claudemux system, MCP integration, session management, fetch module, go-git integration. Ensure diagrams are accurate. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T169: docs/architecture-claude-mux.md refresh
sequentialTasks.description: Update docs/architecture-claude-mux.md to reflect actual implementation state after T110-T114. Remove speculative content. Add concrete PTY spawn and Pool dispatch documentation. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T170: Final cross-platform verification — macOS
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make make-all-with-log' on macOS. Zero failures. Document results.

sequentialTasks.task: T171: Final cross-platform verification — Linux
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make make-all-in-container' for Linux via Docker. Zero failures. Document results.

sequentialTasks.task: T172: Final cross-platform verification — Windows
sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make make-all-run-windows' on host moo. Zero failures. Document results.

sequentialTasks.task: T173: Final CHANGELOG update
sequentialTasks.description: Final comprehensive CHANGELOG.md update covering ALL work since last release. Verify completeness — every added feature, every fix, every breaking change documented. Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0 format. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T174: Final Rule of Two commit gate
sequentialTasks.description: Execute Rule of Two: subagent #1 reviews full diff vs main. If PASS: freeze scope, subagent #2 reviews same diff. Both PASS: commit with Kubernetes-style message. Either FAIL: fix, reset counter to 0, restart gate.

sequentialTasks.task: T175: Scope expansion — identify next improvement frontier
sequentialTasks.description: After T174 passes, step back and identify the NEXT frontier of improvements. Add new tasks to blueprint. Areas to consider: performance optimization, new JS modules, additional goals, UI/UX refinements, new MCP tools, telemetry, plugin system. The blueprint must NEVER be empty.

continuousVerification.task: Run 'make make-all-with-log' after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP and fix. Zero tolerance.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Target 100%. Verify with go tool cover.
continuousVerification.crossPlatform: Checkpoints at T120, T136, T160, T170-T172.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: Use config.mk custom targets. Limit output. Tee to build.log.
No timing-dependent tests. No long-running tests.
Continually refine. After every task fix stale descriptions.
Two contiguous reviews. Same diff. Reset on any code change.
NEVER add .deadcodeignore entries. Wire up or delete.
Run make after EVERY change. Zero failures before proceeding.
